title: Build and qualify a read-only Claude benchmark runner
goal: Implement the smallest isolated Claude runner that consumes the merged Lenskit benchmark request, exposes only the registered read-only baseline or RepoBrief treatment tools, preserves the complete provider stream, and emits a Lenskit-compatible receipt with exact model, provider-reported usage and normalized tool calls before any real benchmark batch is dispatched.

approval.note: Implement and test the runner with synthetic provider streams only. A live Claude preflight or the 96-run benchmark requires a separate explicit reviewed dispatch with bounded spend.

acceptance.assertion: The runner reads exactly one bounded Lenskit agent-benchmark request from stdin, validates request identity, condition, commit, budgets, isolation fields and treatment binding, and rejects unknown or post-hoc fields.

Each request gets a create-only private checkout of the exact registered commit with a unique session/workspace identity; source repositories, prior sessions, transcripts and caches are not reused across conditions.

Baseline exposes only read, glob and grep capabilities mapped to the registered abstract tools; treatment exposes the same tools plus only the request-bound RepoBrief stdio MCP server. Write, shell, web, memory and unrelated MCP tools are absent.
Claude runs non-interactively with stream-json, verbose events, no session persistence and an explicit model; the complete bounded JSONL stream is stored create-only and hash-bound as the transcript.

The runner requires the provider system/init model identity and cumulative result usage, records provider-reported input/output tokens without estimation, and invalidates missing, negative, contradictory or non-integer usage.

Tool-use events and corresponding results are paired by provider tool-use id, mapped to the Lenskit tool vocabulary, ordered deterministically, bounded by request budgets and retained in the immutable transcript.

The final structured provider result is normalized into the Lenskit answer contract with outcome, reported paths, symbols, citations, claims and evidence sufficiency; malformed or absent structured output makes the run invalid.
Synthetic stream fixtures prove parsing, isolation and fail-closed behavior but cannot establish provider availability, real agent usefulness, benchmark success or default promotion.

Focused and full Grabowski tests cover baseline, treatment, unknown tools, transcript limits, duplicate ids, missing result, usage drift, checkout mismatch, MCP binding, timeout and process failure; all repository CI gates pass before merge.

parallel_exception.reason: The visible open Grabowski PR #179 changes MCP initialization, deployment tooling and generated operator context. The runner task uses new competition-independent files. The active Heimlern outcome task concerns routing-outcome production and Chronik transport; no matching public branch, PR or file was found in the runner scope.
parallel_exception.remaining_uncertainty: Unpublished local worktrees and leases are not visible through GitHub and must be re-read by the execution broker before mutation.

provider_contract_basis: Claude Code stream-json system/init model and tools
Claude Agent SDK result usage and per-model usage
